jobs.company_name: Servicios Comerciales Amazon Mexico S. de R.L. de C.V.
jobs.description:
Amazon is looking for high-potential leaders, who are ready to own their impact in Amazon Operations as an entry-level manager. We promise you will have your leadership capacity stretched to its full potential. As a front-line leader, you will have an opportunity to truly invest in others and develop a people-focused leadership style, while mastering the tools, processes, and operations that have created the most customer-centric company on Earth. If you want to be involved in continuing to define the future of online retail, and are dynamic and an organized self-starter, join our team in Amazon Operations as an Area Manager.

Key job responsibilities
As an Area Manager, you will have the opportunity to lead and develop your own team of Amazon Associates in a distribution warehouse environment. Your main focus will be motivating, mentoring and coaching your team. As a servant leader, the Area Manager will ensure that their team of Amazon Associates have all the tools needed to succeed for any task during a shift. Engaging with your team during a shift to ensure that the highest levels of safety, quality, attendance performance and engagement are maintained are core functions of the role. Come learn how the largest online retailer in the world runs its fulfillment and delivery networks!

What is fulfillment you ask? Fulfillment is how we refer to completing or fulfilling a customer's Amazon.com order and the acts of picking, packing, shipping and delivering their order to meet or exceed their expectations!

About the team
Inclusive team culture
At Amazon we embrace our differences and we are committed to furthering our culture of inclusion. We host annual and ongoing learning experiences. Amazon’s culture of inclusion is reinforced within our 14 Leadership Principles, which remind team members to seek diverse perspectives, learn and be curious, and earn trust.
 
Work Life Balance
We believe striking the right balance between your personal and professional life is critical to life-long happiness and fulfillment. We offer flexibility in working hours and encourage you to find your own balance between your work and personal lives.
 
Mentorship & Career Growth
Our team is dedicated to supporting new members. We have a broad mix of experience levels and tenures, and we’re building an environment that celebrates knowledge sharing and mentorship. We care about your career growth and strive to assign projects based on what will help each team member develop into a better-rounded professional.

jobs.description:
Amazon is seeking for an Area Manager Jr. for one our Fulfillment Centers.

Four key areas that you’ll always focus on are the safety, quality, customer experience, and productivity of your department:

· Review the work forecasts and determine your productivity requirements to produce during the day to meet the overall building objectives
· Partner with other Area Managers to balance labor ensuring that you are operating a balanced and efficient shift while meeting all of your goals
· Support all safety programs and OSHA compliance to ensure a safe work environment for all associates
· Proactively identify and lead process improvement initiatives and Lean tools

Key job responsibilities
Supervisory Responsibilities:
· You and your team of warehouse associates are responsible for getting Amazon customers their orders. You are responsible for ensuring that you have properly trained people, and that their needs are addressed so they can focus on their jobs.
· You take ownership in your work and team and are available to work overtime during Amazon’s peak holiday season and as needed year-round. You are obsessed with customer experience and understand the importance of a 24/7 operations. You are flexible to work on any type of assigned shift (nights, weekends, etc.)
· Leading and developing a team of 50-150 hourly associates by embodying Amazon’s Leadership Principles
· Proactively identifying and leading process improvement initiatives using Lean tools to delight customers

· In addition, one of the most important aspects of your job is to lead change at internet speed……innovation has made us the global company that we are today.

· Additional Job Elements:
· Lift and move totes up to 49 pounds each
· Regular bending, lifting, stretching and reaching both below the waist and above the head
· Walking in the FC and around area with great frequency; facilities are over a quarter mile in length
· Must be able to stand/walk for up to 10-12 hours
· Should be able to work in environments with variable noise levels, lighting conditions and temperature variation
· Able to access all areas of building (ascending and descending ladders, stairs, gangways safely and without limitation.

About the team
Inclusive team culture
At Amazon we embrace our differences and we are committed to furthering our culture of inclusion. We host annual and ongoing learning experiences. Amazon’s culture of inclusion is reinforced within our 14 Leadership Principles, which remind team members to seek diverse perspectives, learn and be curious, and earn trust.
 
Work Life Balance
We believe striking the right balance between your personal and professional life is critical to life-long happiness and fulfillment. We offer flexibility in working hours and encourage you to find your own balance between your work and personal lives.
 
Mentorship & Career Growth
Our team is dedicated to supporting new members. We have a broad mix of experience levels and tenures, and we’re building an environment that celebrates knowledge sharing and mentorship. We care about your career growth and strive to assign projects based on what will help each team member develop into a better-rounded professional.

jobs.description:
This role serves as a foundational HR partner supporting site operations through consistent execution of PXT programs and relationship-building with hourly employees and frontline leaders. This role maintains visible floor presence to foster team culture, drive engagement, and coach leaders on people management practices. Key responsibilities include supporting employee experience initiatives, executing recognition and inclusion programs, facilitating voice of employee channels, and coaching managers through performance conversations and talent processes.

Builds business acumen while partnering with senior PXT team members to reinforce Amazon values, monitor organizational health metrics (attrition, attendance, engagement), and support talent planning activities. This position requires strong judgment in conflict resolution, policy guidance, and escalation awareness. Success is measured by supporting healthy site metrics, reinforcing manager routines, and creating positive employee experiences while developing skills in influencing without authority.

Key job responsibilities
Employee Experience & Culture:

-Maintain visible floor presence to build rapport with employees and leaders
-Coach frontline leaders on behaviors that improve engagement and retention
-Facilitate employee feedback channels (roundtables, pulse checks, Voice of Employee)
-Coordinate recognition activities and inclusion initiatives
-Support conflict resolution by facilitating conversations and gathering facts
-Promote self-service tools for routine questions

Strategic PXT Partnership:

-Partner with frontline and mid-level leaders to reinforce people leadership expectations
-Influence without authority through relationship-building and clear communication
-Monitor patterns that signal emerging issues and escalate appropriately
-Reinforce Amazon policies and PXT standards
-Support onboarding coordination for new employees and leaders
-Assist with internal investigations while maintaining confidentiality

Talent Planning & Organizational Health:

-Coach managers through performance conversations and documentation
-Support talent processes including calibrations, promotions, and development discussions
-Monitor attendance, attrition, and engagement trends
-Recommend development resources based on observed gaps

PXT Operational Excellence:

-Execute consistent daily and weekly routines
-Track and interpret PXT reports and employee feedback data
-Collaborate with PXT team members to deliver consistent employee experience

About the team
Inclusive team culture
At Amazon we embrace our differences and we are committed to furthering our culture of inclusion. We host annual and ongoing learning experiences. Amazon’s culture of inclusion is reinforced within our 14 Leadership Principles, which remind team members to seek diverse perspectives, learn and be curious, and earn trust.
 
Work Life Balance
We believe striking the right balance between your personal and professional life is critical to life-long happiness and fulfillment. We offer flexibility in working hours and encourage you to find your own balance between your work and personal lives.
 
Mentorship & Career Growth
Our team is dedicated to supporting new members. We have a broad mix of experience levels and tenures, and we’re building an environment that celebrates knowledge sharing and mentorship. We care about your career growth and strive to assign projects based on what will help each team member develop into a better-rounded professional.

jobs.description:
For more than 11 years, Amazon Web Services (“AWS”) has been the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. AWS offers over 100 fully featured services to millions of active customers around the world, including the fastest-growing startups, largest enterprises, and leading government agencies, to power their infrastructure. This role will be within the larger AWS Sales and Marketing Operations team.

The Contract Specialist will support AWS Professional Services and AWS Legal by operationalizing the contract creation process for Statements of Work (SOWs) and related contracting structures. We're looking for a motivated self-starter who's passionate about drafting and reviewing contracts, managing operational processes, analyzing trends and recommending and implementing process improvements.

This is a unique role, with similar functions to a business operations analyst and a contract analyst. The candidate will work side by side with AWS Legal to create SOWs and ensure they meet legal requirements. Additionally, you will support AWS Professional Services by providing operational and administrative guidance, enabling them to provide an outstanding customer experience.

The successful candidate must be inquisitive, enthusiastic about technology, like being continually challenged, demonstrate sound judgment, and attention to detail. You should have an interest in working in a space, where legal meets business.


This position may be based in our AWS offices in Seattle, WA; Dallas, TX; and Arlington, VA






Key job responsibilities
- Own the SOW process, which includes drafting and reviewing SOWs to ensure alignment with business and legal requirements, gathering approvals, tracking status and communication between key stakeholders
- Proactively identify and implement operational improvements and enhancements that meet business requirements
- Communicate daily with cross functional teams including Sales, Legal, Operations and Management on assigned deals
- Recruit, onboard, train and influence other Contract Specialists
- Create and maintain standard operating procedures
- Drive continuous process improvement while developing relationships for long term solutions
- Become the SOW creation subject matter expert


About the team
About AWS

Diverse Experiences
AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

Why AWS?
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.

Inclusive Team Culture
Here at AWS, it’s in our nature to learn and be curious. Our employee-led affinity groups foster a culture of inclusion that empower us to be proud of our differences. Ongoing events and learning experiences, including our Conversations on Race and Ethnicity (CORE) and AmazeCon (gender diversity) conferences, inspire us to never stop embracing our uniqueness.

Mentorship & Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve in the cloud.

About Sales, Marketing and Global Services (SMGS)

AWS Sales, Marketing, and Global Services (SMGS) is responsible for driving revenue, adoption, and growth from the largest and fastest growing small- and mid-market accounts to enterprise-level customers including public sector.

jobs.company_name: Servicios Comerciales Amazon Mexico S. de R.L. de C.V.
jobs.description:
Our Senior Operations Managers play a crucial role in exceeding expectations and providing a superb customer experience; critical to Amazon’s success which is built on a foundation of customer obsession, and innovation. This position is a multi-faceted role requiring the ability to balance strategy and execution. The Senior Operations Manager is responsible for all aspects of the operations of a functional area for one of our Fulfillment Centers including, people management and development, process, and meeting or exceeding the expectations and promise made to our customers. You will lead a dynamic team of Operations and Area Managers to achieve operational excellence through coaching and mentoring the team; driving employee engagement, and building leadership bench strength within the Fulfillment Center.

As a senior leader you will oversee upwards of 1,000+ salaried and hourly employees, in a fast-paced, complex operations facility up to a million square feet.

Our Senior Operations Managers serve as the face of the organization to potentially thousands of employees and the community where the center is located. This position offers unlimited career potential as we continue to grow our global footprint.



Key job responsibilities
•Owns and delivers the operational budget for functional area, including safety, productivity, financial planning, labor planning, and operational goals.
•Responsible for providing strategic level/long-term planning (3, 6, and 12 months out), including labor planning, rate forecasting, and peak season planning.
•Drives continuous improvement projects to optimize operations and improve productivity to meet and exceed business objectives; works on strategic projects that have total building and network-wide impact.
•Effectively leverages Operations and Area Managers to solicit ideas and understand problems and challenges in the building.
•Networks with other leaders across the network to solicit and share process improvements and drive efficiencies and cost savings.
•Establishes objectives and metrics for safety, quality, productivity, and customer experience.
•Sets clear goals and expectations for Operations and Area Managers; measures performance, provides feedback, and holds leaders accountable.
•Mentors and develops leaders and staff.
•Participates in recruiting to identify and evaluate associates and internal/external candidates for promotion and hire.
•Works closely with support staff (HR, Finance, •Maintenance Engineering, Safety, IT, and other leaders) to build and secure support and resources for projects and initiatives.

About the team
Inclusive team culture
At Amazon we embrace our differences and we are committed to furthering our culture of inclusion. We host annual and ongoing learning experiences. Amazon’s culture of inclusion is reinforced within our 14 Leadership Principles, which remind team members to seek diverse perspectives, learn and be curious, and earn trust.
 
Work Life Balance
We believe striking the right balance between your personal and professional life is critical to life-long happiness and fulfillment. We offer flexibility in working hours and encourage you to find your own balance between your work and personal lives.
 
Mentorship & Career Growth
Our team is dedicated to supporting new members. We have a broad mix of experience levels and tenures, and we’re building an environment that celebrates knowledge sharing and mentorship. We care about your career growth and strive to assign projects based on what will help each team member develop into a better-rounded professional.

jobs.description:
Transform network infrastructure by joining our dynamic team! You'll be at the forefront of developing innovative solutions that drive network stability and scalability, using your technical expertise to create automated systems that power global technological advancement.

InfraDelivery Install Technicians will collaborate with cross-functional teams, troubleshoot complex technical scenarios, and implement strategic solutions that keep our network running seamlessly. Expect to engage in hands-on technical work that requires creativity, precision, and a passion for technological innovation.
AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help.
You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion.
NOTE: Amazon does not sponsor for immigration, including for H-1B, TN, and other non-immigrant visas, for this role.
NOTE: Lump sum stipend will be provided to eligible candidates who relocate for this position.

Physical Requirements:
• Regularly lift and/or move up to 40 pounds; and participate in group lifts for 41+ pounds
• Ability to work in an environment that operates 24/7 with an ability to participate in primary responder rotation and provide after-hours support as needed
• Working in cramped and/or elevated and/or noisy environments
• Bending, lifting, stretching, reaching, standing and walking for up to 8+ hours a day
• Ascending and descending ladders, stairs, and gangways safely and without limitation
This role supports data centers in various operational stages. The position may be assigned to a facility still under development, which could require travel to established locations for training until your designated site becomes fully operational.
Essential Requirements:
• Ability to travel to or commute between data center locations as needed
• Willingness to temporarily work at alternative sites during training periods or until assigned facility is operational
• Travel frequency will vary based on business needs and operational status of assigned facility
Candidates must be able to travel (over 50 miles) or commute (less than 50 miles) to active data center sites as required by business needs.



Key job responsibilities
• Moving and implementing large enterprise equipment
• Installing Fiber and Copper cabling and patching cabling between equipment
• Ensuring proper safety and security standards are maintained during cabling and rack installation,
• Following well established hardware installation procedures and break-fix activities
• Safely using material handling tools and equipment
• Traveling within and outside of the regional work area is required. You will be responsible for having a reliable personal vehicle and valid driver's license to travel within the regional work area, as company transportation will not be provided

- Travel up to 50% domestic travel with short notice
jobs.description:
Amazon は、Amazon の物流拠点 (カスタマーフルフィルメント、Amazon Transportation Services、Amazon ロジスティクス、またはグローバルスペシャリティフルフィルメント) の大量採用プログラムをリードする意欲のあるスタッフィングリード (リクルーター/調達) を募集しています。
スタッフィングリードは、物流拠点での円滑な入出荷を実現するための人員確保をリードします。
主要なビジネス パートナーとともに Workforce Staffing (WFS) のオペレーショナル エクセレンスを推進するために、高いモチベーションと柔軟性を持っています。
成功する候補者は、優れたコンサルティングスキル、優れた影響力、組織の専門知識、口頭および書面による優れたコミュニケーションスキル、事業運営を改善するデータ主導型プログラムの構築への情熱を示す必要があります。
また多少の不確実さがあっても柔軟に受け入れることができ、最高クラスのプログラムを開発する上では、革新的であることが求められます。
・採用計画の立案や募集（求人広告等の出稿）を企画・実行するチームに対して最適な情報をインプットします。
・物流拠点運営責任者との定期的な会議を通じて、人員確保の進捗を共有します。問題が発生した場合は、チームで協力して解決にあたります。
・スタッフィングリードは、派遣会社との交渉を通じて、必要な人員確保を促進するとともに、Amazonで直接採用をした人材の充足の役割を担います。
·隣接する物流拠点を複数担当していただきます。物流拠点での採用イベント・内定者イベント等をリードしますので、日帰り・宿泊の出張が発生します。（現在は原則オンラインで実施しています）
·業務はマイクロソフトオフィスを使用します。特にエクセルとワードは日常的に使用します。
エクセルは、ピボットテーブル、IF関数、VLOOKUP関数等を使用してデータ分析を行います。
また、報告や提案など様々な場面において、ワードで文書を作成しています。
· 問題解決と分析に厳密なアプローチを取り、データから結論を導き出すとともに、ステークホルダーやオペレーションチームに、専門知識と技術的もしくは実行可能なガイダンスを提供します。
· ボトルネックの予測、エスカレーションの管理、トレードオフの予測と判断、ビジネスニーズと制約のバランスを取ります。
·ビジネスの優先順位を確立し、ステークホルダーと協力してリスクと緩和策を特定、伝達し、目標、マイルストーン、完了日を策定、維持します。
·上級レベルのステークホルダーにアイデアを提示し、フィードバックを受け入れ、変更をすばやく適用します。
·機密情報や重要な情報をすぐに判断して扱う能力
·実務スキルと知的スキルを兼ね備え、また、複雑な分析、実践的なプロジェクト、プロジェクト管理などの様々な業務を迅速にこなし、適応できる能力
·
業務上、英語を使用する機会があります。海外のチームとの定期的な会議への参加やレポート作成、メール等で使用する機会があります。また、海外のチームとの連携は業務遂行上とても重要です。

主な責任には以下が含まれますが、これらに限定されません。
· 採用プロセスにおける潜在的な問題を特定し、是正措置/エスカレーションが完了していることを確認する
· オペレーション指標を深く掘り下げて、パフォーマンスの向上、不具合のトラブルシューティング、目標達成のための是正措置の策定を行う。
· オペレーション計画に携わるビジネスアナリストと直接対話し、指標の方法論と検証に関する議論を行う 。
· 顧客/パートナーのニーズとタスク分析を行い、プロジェクトの範囲を定め、既存のプロセスを評価してソリューションを提案する。

（キャリアパス）
複数のスタッフィングリードをまとめるマネージャーが最も身近なキャリアパスです。
Amazonでは自分のキャリアは自分で開発するという文化が根付いています。社内公募制も年間を通じて運用されており、様々なキャリアのチャンスが提供されています。

Amazon is looking for staffing leads (recruiters) that are motivated to lead mass recruitment programs for Amazon's logistics sites (Customer Fulfillment, Amazon Transportation Services, Amazon Logistics, or Global Specialty Fulfillment). Staffing Leads take the lead in securing personnel to achieve smooth receipt and shipment at logistics hubs. They are highly motivated and flexible to drive operational excellence for Workforce Staffing (WFS) with its major business partners.

A successful candidate must demonstrate strong consulting skills, exceptional ability to influence, organizational expertise, strong verbal and written communication skills, and a passion for building data driven programs that improve business operations. They are self-motivated, solutions-oriented, comfortable with ambiguity, and innovative in developing best-in-class programs.

Key Responsibilities include, but are not limited to:
Strategic Workforce Planning & Execution
• Shape the future of workforce strategy by developing and executing comprehensive staffing plans aligned with business objectives, validating Labor Orders (Hiring Requirements) per guardrails, and participating in capacity planning calls
• Drive data-driven decision making by analyzing hiring trends, attrition patterns, and workforce metrics to uncover opportunities, deep dive operational performance to troubleshoot defects, and create remedial action plans that consistently deliver results
• Lead continuous improvement initiatives that optimize hiring efficiency, quality, and speed-to-fill while reducing cost-per-hire through systematic process enhancements and automation
• Partner strategically with external staffing vendor managers on site-specific hiring and conversion strategies for business-critical locations, aligning on execution plans
• Navigate complexity with agility by anticipating bottlenecks, providing escalation management, making strategic tradeoffs, and balancing competing business needs versus constraints
Daily Operational Leadership & Governance
• Lead daily operational excellence through site-specific huddles focused on capacity optimization and labor hour management, while facilitating Staffing Lead and Staffing Associate daily standup meetings for issue resolution and strategic alignment
• Serve as the go-to expert for day-to-day staffing inquiries from Operations teams and vendor partners, proactively identifying and resolving potential hiring process issues through systematic monitoring
• Engage in strategic governance through active participation in RLC and escalation reviews, ensuring corrective actions are completed timely
• Build executive relationships through regular 1-1 meetings with senior leadership for strategic guidance, alignment, and decision-making support
• Establish clear priorities while identifying risks early, developing mitigation plans, and communicating business priorities in close collaboration with stakeholders
Team Leadership & Development
• Build and inspire high-performing teams by leading, mentoring, and developing Staffing Associates across multiple sites through regular 1-1 meetings, meaningful performance reviews, and personalized career development discussions
• Drive accountability and results by establishing clear team goals, tracking performance metrics, holding team accountable for outcomes, and celebrating achievements
• Develop future leaders by identifying training needs and creating capability-building programs that elevate team performance and prepare associates for advancement
• Cultivate a winning culture rooted in customer obsession, ownership, innovation, and continuous improvement across all team interactions and initiatives
Blue Badge (Direct) Hiring Leadership
• Champion direct hiring initiatives by leading referral events, HelloWork partnerships, conversion programs, and in-person New Hire Events (NHE) at sites to create memorable candidate experiences
• Connect with the frontline and community through regular Gemba walks to generate referrals and gather associate feedback, while visiting HelloWork offices to strengthen partnerships and expand candidate sourcing
• Drive innovative sourcing strategies by collaborating with Operations, Agencies, and Marketing teams to identify conversion candidates and optimize both paid and non-paid candidate attraction channels
• Expand diverse talent pipelines through specialized channels including foreign workers, PWD (Persons with Disabilities), and DEI initiatives, while forging community connections through partnerships with vocational schools and community organizations
Green Badge (Contractual) Workforce Management
• Orchestrate vendor coordination and regional alignment through weekly meetings to confirm Labor Order allocation, facilitate Operations and UTR team updates, and ensure operational readiness
• Execute Path to Green (PTG) excellence by onboarding new agencies, strengthening existing partnerships, optimizing shift allocation strategies, and managing vendor relationships effectively
• Ensure system accuracy and operational excellence by resolving Octomate staff rostering errors, handling Beeline system issues, maintaining data integrity, and monitoring Green Badge rostering accuracy
• Optimize workforce utilization by tracking attendance patterns, productivity metrics, and workforce performance while driving retention initiatives that reduce early attrition and enhance associate engagement
Stakeholder Management & External Partnerships
• Serve as a strategic business partner to Site Operations leadership on all workforce planning matters, conducting regular site-agency visits for coordination and partnership development
• Influence senior leadership by presenting compelling workforce insights, hiring forecasts, and strategic recommendations while collaborating with Business Analysts on operational planning and metric methodology
• Build cross-functional excellence by partnering with HR, Finance, Learning & Development, and Safety teams to deliver integrated workforce solutions
• Ensure compliance and integrity with labor laws, agency contracts, and Amazon policies across all operations while handling sensitive matters professionally with appropriate discretion and confidentiality
Systems & Data Management
• Own data integrity and system excellence by ensuring accuracy in HRMS systems for all associates, overseeing proactive maintenance, and driving rapid error resolution for workforce management tools
• Transform data into insights by leveraging analytics to generate actionable intelligence, inform strategic decisions, and perform customer/partner needs analysis
• Champion automation and innovation by implementing process improvements, evaluating existing processes, scoping projects, and proposing solutions that enhance operational efficiency

A day in the life
The WFS Team has an environment where team members collaborate, support each other, and learn together. As a Staffing Lead, you will balance strategic planning with hands-on execution, conducting daily huddles with operations, leading your team through coaching and development, executing hiring events on the floor, managing vendor relationships, and presenting workforce insights. You will travel to multiple assigned sites for hiring events and stakeholder meetings (day trips and overnight stays may be required). You will use Microsoft Office applications daily, particularly Excel and Word. You will work with global teams in English through meetings, reports, and emails

About the team
The Workforce Staffing (WFS) department, part of a global team, is seeking a teammate who can think from a customer-centric perspective and create further innovation in WFS as a Staffing Lead. This role primarily supports different operations divisions across Japan including Customer Fulfillment, Amazon Transportation Services, Amazon Logistics, and Global Specialty Fulfillment.

jobs.company_name: Servicios Comerciales Amazon Mexico S. de R.L. de C.V.
jobs.description:
The HRBP provides support to a Fulfillment Center Operations team by partnering with senior stakeholders on the site related business and people agenda and manages the site's wide projects independently.

COMPANY CULTURE STEWARD:
- Applies understanding of Leadership Principles and values when consulting with employees and managers (i.e.: performance improvement, coaching plans, team meetings, employee development and promotions)
- Initiates and supports client group or site (or multiple site leadership) to develop the culture for that site or business

ORGANIZATION DEVELOPMENT & PLANNING
- Understands key business goals, and business priorities, able to apply to core areas of HR responsibilities in order to prioritize work and set goals
- Provides input into org design discussions (i.e. information about talent for succession planning, attrition risks)
Implements communication plan for org changes

ORGANIZATION HEALTH
- Conducts diagnostics to identify trends & environmental ER or workplace issues, communicates findings
- Partners with management to drive positive ER, or negotiation of works council or labor agreements
- Proactively monitors and acts to address ER or labor changes for the operation
- Implements components of strategy and action plans that addresses workplace environment, employee engagement or retention issues
- Conducts investigations of employee relations issues in consultation with management
- Supports rollout of compliance initiatives; understands and independently counsels the business regarding local employment law and legislation changes
- Leads client group or site engagement activities and audits to monitor org health and to engage with employees/associates

WORKFORCE PLANNING & TALENT ACQUISITION
- Partners with Recruiting and Agencies to coach managers around recruiting process
- Interprets hiring data and provides recommendations to recruiting and client leaders for sourcing strategies
- Understands local market for staffing needs
- Participates in interview loops and debriefs
- Approves offers, transfers, and compensation exceptions in client org
- Engages with managers to ensure launch plans are created, participates in launch plans as needed
- Monitors vendor and 3rd party worker relationships for co-employment risk
- Works closely with staffing and/or recruiting partners to maintain healthy candidate/staffing funnels
- May manage vendor relationships, including facilities temp agencies, maintenance, drivers, and security

TALENT MANAGEMENT & DEVELOPMENT
- Facilitates or assists multiple department or group level talent discussions
- Drives identification and management of forward looking promotions for managers
- Coaches and develops business, site(s) leadership on performance and talent management with the business teams
- Identifies participants for programs, and manages internal communications related to training and development
- Partners with leaders on talent development

HR OPERATIONS
- Analyzes data and shares with leaders
- Trains and/or consults with managers on employee life cycle issues
- Utilizes Connections data and provides ideas and strategies for how to improve engagement and manager capability
- Is able to speak to attendance, attrition, and staffing trends
- Identifies gaps in policy and services, makes recommendations for system process improvement and may draft and maintain policies for site
- Rolls out new policy changes/services and ensures business adoption and/or understanding of changes
- May recommend exceptions to policies with input from senior leaders or HR management.

#AmazonMexico

About the team
Inclusive team culture
At Amazon we embrace our differences and we are committed to furthering our culture of inclusion. We host annual and ongoing learning experiences. Amazon’s culture of inclusion is reinforced within our 14 Leadership Principles, which remind team members to seek diverse perspectives, learn and be curious, and earn trust.
 
Work Life Balance
We believe striking the right balance between your personal and professional life is critical to life-long happiness and fulfillment. We offer flexibility in working hours and encourage you to find your own balance between your work and personal lives.
 
Mentorship & Career Growth
Our team is dedicated to supporting new members. We have a broad mix of experience levels and tenures, and we’re building an environment that celebrates knowledge sharing and mentorship. We care about your career growth and strive to assign projects based on what will help each team member develop into a better-rounded professional.

jobs.company_name: Servicios Comerciales Amazon Mexico S. de R.L. de C.V.
jobs.description:
Our MXCF Operations network delivers millions of packages and smiles to Amazon customers every day. We are looking for seasoned, customer-focused leaders who want to join our team as an Area Manager at our new Inbound Cross Dock (IXD) - the first of its kind in Mexico. This state-of-the-art facility represents a significant milestone in Amazon's expansion in Latin America, featuring new processes and innovative cross-docking solutions.

In this role, you will lead multiple teams of hourly workforce and will be responsible for developing and coaching other managers while ensuring the highest levels of safety, quality, attendance, and performance across operations. You will drive strategic initiatives and optimize processes while maintaining service level agreements and delivery expectations. Your scope includes ensuring inventory is efficiently processed and distributed from vendors to our Fulfillment Centers (FCs) at the right time and in the right quantities, supporting the broader Amazon fulfillment network and ultimately enabling timely customer deliveries. You will also be responsible for implementing new technologies and processes to enhance operational efficiency.

Key job responsibilities
- Lead multiple departments/areas of hourly associates through effective coaching, mentoring, and performance management.
- Drive strategic initiatives and implement innovative solutions to improve operational efficiency across multiple areas/shifts.
- Analyze complex data and metrics to identify trends, develop action plans, and drive continuous improvement projects that impact multiple departments.
- Manage safety, quality, productivity, and customer delivery promise at a broader scope while ensuring standardization across operations.
- Build and maintain strong partnerships with all support teams including Safety, Engineering, Loss Prevention, Quality Assurance, Human Resources to develop and execute long-term strategies.
- Create and maintain performance management tools to drive accountability.
- Lift up to 49 pounds and frequently push, pull, squat, bend, and reach.
- Stand/walk for up to 12 hours during shifts
- Work in an environment where the noise level varies and can be loud.
- Work in an environment that is subject to variable temperatures and weather.
- Continuously climb and descend stairs.(applies to sites with stairs)

A day in the life
A typical day might look like this:
- Begin with a comprehensive review of cross-departmental metrics and KPIs from previous shifts.
- Lead multiple area huddles to align goals, safety priorities, and operational strategies.
- Conduct Gemba walks across various departments to identify process improvement opportunities and ensure standardization.
- Drive problem-solving sessions to address systemic issues affecting multiple areas.
- Develop and implement process improvements to optimize productivity and quality.
- Lead critical projects that impact multiple operational areas.

You'll be instrumental in shaping the future of our operations while maintaining day-to-day excellence across various departments.

About the team
We're launching an exciting new operation in Mexico - our first Inbound Cross Dock (IXD) in the country! This represents a significant milestone in Amazon's growth in Latin America, and you'll be part of the founding team that will shape the future of our upstream operations in the region.

This team will play a crucial role in Amazon's supply chain network, managing the efficient flow of vendor and sellers shipments to multiple Fulfillment Centers across Mexico. We're focused on operational excellence, innovative solutions, and continuous improvement as we establish this key node in our logistics network that will streamline the receiving and distribution process of the inventory products.

If you're energized by the idea of being a founding member of a new operation and want to make your mark on Amazon's growth in Mexico, we want to hear from you!

Inclusive team culture
At Amazon we embrace our differences and we are committed to furthering our culture of inclusion. We host annual and ongoing learning experiences. Amazon’s culture of inclusion is reinforced within our 14 Leadership Principles, which remind team members to seek diverse perspectives, learn and be curious, and earn trust.

Work Life Balance
We believe striking the right balance between your personal and professional life is critical to life-long happiness and fulfillment. We offer flexibility in working hours and encourage you to find your own balance between your work and personal lives.

Mentorship & Career Growth
Our team is dedicated to supporting new members. We have a broad mix of experience levels and tenures, and we’re building an environment that celebrates knowledge sharing and mentorship. We care about your career growth and strive to assign projects based on what will help each team member develop into a better-rounded professional.
